sections.paragraph:
καταφθείρω fut. καταφθερῶ 2 Ch 17:7; aor. κατέφθειρα LXX; pf. κατέφθαρκα LXX. Pass.: 2 fut. καταφθαρήσομαι; 2 aor. κατεφθάρην; pf. ptc. κατεφθαρμένος (s. next entry and φθείρω; Aeschyl., Pla., et al.; ins, pap, LXX; TestAsh 7:2; EpArist; Philo, Deus Imm. 141; 142; Just., D. 31, 5 [Da 7:19]).

① destroy (Aeschyl., LXX) pass. be destroyed (Polyb. 2, 64, 3; Lev 26:39; 2 Km 14:14 v.l.; 2 Macc 5:14) 2 Pt 2:12 v.l. (cp. the portent Plut., Demetr. 894 [12, 3]).

② ruin, corrupt τινά someone; of grief παρὰ πάντα τὰ πνεύματα κ. τὸν ἄνθρωπον it ruins a man more than all the (other) spirits Hm 10, 1, 2. Pass. be ruined, become useless (SIG 1157, 74 [I b.c.] δένδρα; pap) ἀπό τινος for someth. ἀπὸ τῶν βιωτικῶν πράξεων for the duties of everyday life Hv 1, 3, 1b; cp. Hs 6, 2, 4. κ. ὑπό τινος εἴς τι: εἰς θάνατον be injured by someone so that one dies Hs 9, 26, 6. κατεφθαρμένος τὸν νοῦν depraved in mind 2 Ti 3:8 (Menand., Epitr. 692 S. p. 347 [502 Kö.] καταφθαρεὶς τὸν βίον). Abs. Hv 1, 3, 1a; m 10, 1, 4; Hs 9, 14, 3; 9, 26, 4.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestAsh TestAsh = Testament of Asher, s. Test12Patr—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

someth. someth. = something

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

m m = Mandates

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 529
